dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you cannot hear me, but... if you have comforted my father in his plight, thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Three-headed Cerberus permits none approach, neither him nor his master.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The monstrous Hound of Hell bares countless fangs as if to say, back off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Feared watch-dog Cerberus regards the Princess with his six expectant eyes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The multiheaded monster keeps his fury well in check beside the rightful Underworld King.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Fierce hell-hound Cerberus has many fangs but little trust to spare.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The resting hound of hell makes plain he wishes not to be disturbed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Three-headed Cerberus eyes every surface of the chamber, all at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The savage watch dog looks tentatively at the Princess of the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Notoriously vicious Cerberus sits vigilantly by his master's side.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The triple-headed guardian of hell does not wish to be troubled at this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The brutal hound of hell by now permits the Princess of the Dead to approach.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Where goes the lord and master of the dead, the infernal watchdog of the Underworld follows.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The one-time guardian of the gates of hell restrains his viciousness for the Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: For unknown reasons does this savage beast decide not to devour the Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Somehow the Princess of the Underworld has worked her magick on the hound of hell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The Infernal Beast that wrought such terror in the Mourning Fields now sits there calmly and expectantly, not for the Princess of the Underworld, but someone else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Ferocious Cerberus yet guards his master dutifully, having discovered him within these depths.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The notoriously fearsome triple-headed monster Cerberus rests in his favored spot, and now regards the Princess of the Dead with three expressions different than before.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well boy, this must be the next best thing to guarding the Underworld's gates. Say, do I have your permission to give you a scratch? The way my brother demonstrated before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I used to have a sense for all the blessings of our kin; their Boons were not permitted in this realm. That sense has dulled, for only now do I detect the influence of Ares here...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your sense for surface blessings is intact, Father. I met Ares only after all the rest. It seems this war has taken such a turn that he's now helping put a stop to it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that what he told you...? The only reason I can think of that my nephew would endeavor to conclude a war is if it meant still-greater wars could then be waged instead. Nevertheless, if he is claiming to be on your side, he would not lie outright.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I used to have a sense for all the blessings of our kin; their Boons were not permitted in this realm. That sense has dulled, for only now do I detect the influence of Ares here...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your sense for surface blessings is intact, Father. I met Ares only after all the rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The only reason I can think of that my nephew would endeavor to conclude a war is if it meant still-greater wars could then be waged instead. Nevertheless, if he is claiming to be on your side, he would not lie outright.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: To think that you have seen Olympus with your own eyes... your brother never had that opportunity. And I myself... my life is here, and I've no memories of mountaintops.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It must have been a sight to behold, once. Now it's beset with the Titan's legions, and mindless Automaton defenders that want me destroyed just as much as our foes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I told them never to depend on such contraptions! A proper army needs to be driven by glory, if not fear. Their Automatons feel nothing! How well can they possibly fight?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Where's Cerberus, Father? He's usually right here. I saw him in the Fields as before!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He runs amok at my behest. Spreads the attention of the Titan's legions thin. Eventually they drive him back, but can do him no harm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So just you and the boulder, then. Looking as though it's not been heaved up any hills in quite a while. Chronos doesn't make you do it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd have less difficult a time than a mere Shade. Besides, these shackles cannot be unlocked, save by the Titan's command. But I'd remain here for eternity before I'd grovel at his feet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You say this boulder was companion to a mortal king? I... sense something from it. There can be so much energy within a common stone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll take you at your word. The boulder and I... we're not as close as was its last relationship. Though perhaps you shall coax something from it; the Witch apparently has taught you everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Where's Cerberus, Father? He's usually right here. I saw him in the Fields as before!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He runs amok at my behest. Spreads the attention of the Titan's legions thin. Eventually they drive him back, but can do him no harm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So just you and the boulder, then. Looking as though it's not been heaved up any hills in quite a while. You say this boulder was companion to a mortal king? I... sense something from it. There can be so much energy within a common stone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll take you at your word. The boulder and I... we're not as close as was its last relationship. Though perhaps you shall coax something from it; the Witch apparently has taught you everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Has that boulder yet revealed anything to you? I know not why I ask, mere baseless superstition on my part... but I have seen you go to it from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You spoke gently of the prisoner once held here, as though you had forgiven him. I used to think that was impossible in Tartarus. The boulder now reminds me otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Most of the wretches who we once held captive here had no hope of forgiveness or freedom at all, yet even they broke loose, if undeservedly. Then let the boulder stand as testament to all that has transpired, and shall come to pass.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: My brothers each are aiding you, are they? Zeus, doubtless leading from behind the safety of his palace walls... Poseidon, blustering as though his victory is already at hand...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, they're fighting to defend their home... and they're fighting for you! You're right that neither one is much inclined to show concern, but why should they? Fear spreads.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: They sat oblivious upon their surface realm for far too long...! But do not listen to my rantings about them. Had you known your own brother, perhaps you'd better understand the impulse for such ill-conceived remarks...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What has that blasted Titan done to Cerberus, that he should storm into this place with such abandon, only to be ushered out again? Takes a small army of Satyrs to drive him back...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos casts him out into the Mourning Fields above. Then rage and despair congeal about him, and his better senses take leave. My own path leads me through where he resides.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: So you must face him in that sorry state. Thank you, Daughter, for taking pity on the moody beast. He has seen better, but been through worse. You were too young to form a bond before, but perhaps... it is not too late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus has been quite agitated, and I'm realizing why. It's not just having to roam through the Mourning Fields; he probably can sense Typhon's return.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just about every monster I've confronted probably is traceable to Typhon in some way. I doubt he had a close relationship with any of his kin; not even Cerberus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I doubt that too. It seems not one of us is able to escape our fathers' influence. Yet here we all are, still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The scent of blood. You're injured. And you expect to face the Titan still? At least you're in the right place should you die.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I won't be dying here no matter what. As for my injuries, perhaps Lord Charon has some Life Essence in stock.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Even now, the Boatman plies his trade? Absurd. Our entire operation was shut down! Although... I ought remember that he serves this realm, not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Who else besides Prometheus does Chronos have under his thumb? I would know every last Titan or such ilk who deigned to turn on us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Besides marauding hordes of Satyrs and such, the ones that come to mind include the Cyclops Polyphemus... and Nyx's daughter Eris... though she may be acting on her own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Strife could never be controlled, Nyx knew only too well. As for that Cyclops... he and your Uncle Poseidon have some history that may have spurred his impulses. Well... it is not yet the longest list of enemies, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a cacophony occurred, after you last visited. Exactly when I cannot say. All of the Titan's wretched servants flew into a panic. Their cries would not cease. What transpired?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps they were concerned for their master. Or busy trying to bring him back to life. Because I slew him, finally. And it won't be the last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness, hah... urgh! Then you accomplished... that which once took half the blasted strength of Olympus... all on your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not on my own at all. I had their strength, if not their support; as you've given me yours. Father, we can stop him. And for now... we can kill him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He shall seek vengeance with a renewed zeal. This entire plot of his is driven by the same impulse. Then, kill him if you must. At least... his many deaths ought keep him occupied.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a cacophony occurred, after you last visited. Exactly when I cannot say. All of the Titan's wretched servants flew into a panic. Their cries would not cease. What transpired?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps they were concerned for their master. Or busy trying to bring him back to life. Because I slew him once again. And it won't be the last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness, hah! Urgh... To have achieved such an outcome... which once required half the blasted strength of all Olympus, on your own...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not on my own at all. I had their strength, if not their support; as you've given me yours. Father, we can stop him. And for now... we can kill him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He shall seek vengeance with a renewed zeal. This entire plot of his is driven by the same impulse. Then, kill him if you must. At least... his many deaths ought keep him occupied.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When Chronos took our family and the House, and you bade the Witch of the Crossroads take me... I remember it somehow, or dreamt of it. Did he... demand you tell him of the Fates?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: How could you possibly...? Perhaps the Fates themselves permitted you that bit of insight. My father sought them, but I revealed nothing. Couldn't have even if I so desired, for no one knows where those three are, save them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you never told Chronos how to find the Fates? Then how did he? Because he's captured them. Not long ago, perhaps, but nonetheless. I'm certain of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What?! No... but if this is true, well then my father's plan for conquest is much closer to fruition than I thought. If not even they could stop him... Daughter, what chance do you have...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You saw what happened... what Chronos did to Mother, and my brother, and the rest. Where are they? Where is he holding them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: My knowledge of this is not up-to-date. He... made everybody stop, somehow. I saw them, still as stone; not petrified exactly but ensnared, in the Great Hall of the House. He must have stored them someplace else inside...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even when I'm able to vanquish Chronos on a given night, most of the House is sealed off, and I'm unable to remain for long. How can we break them out...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps if you ask the Titan very nicely, he shall comply? Daughter, I know of no way to undo his work, and have no wisdom to impart. But this, I can provide.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Daughter, tell me what... became of the Witch of the Crossroads, should you know. When all seemed lost... your mother and I, we left you in her care.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And she has cared for me ever since. Prepared me. Though, we had best not speak of her in these chambers. Suffice it to say, the Unseen remain in shadow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good. She was... well as you say, we'd best say nothing more. Though if you have the opportunity, extend to her my deepest gratitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, wanted to ask about your nephew Heracles. In my run-ins with him, he had mentioned something about visiting you and Cerberus here in the Underworld...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Bah, Heracles! He hauled Cerberus bodily up to the surface, did he tell you that? Not by his choice, he claimed, but still! I could not believe my eyes when he hoisted that dog.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You permitted it to happen? Was Cerberus all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at him, of course he was all right. And I permitted that brute only because what he did ought have been impossible! Apparently the incident remains a source of pride.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It may be best to keep your distance there, or rather for that dog. Although... the Witch of the Crossroads, she once had a hound much like that one...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then this must be the very same. She's called Hecuba, and I'm certain she'll be respectful both to you and Cerberus. Besides, they already met in the Fields...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: A creature possessed of loyalty and courage, then. A quite-uncommon combination, whether in beasts or otherwise. Be on your best behavior with her, Cerberus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: If you'd indulge a senseless curiosity... how fares my House? Your brother, he was more or less in charge of the decor. I highly doubt the Titan shares his taste.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, unless Zagreus was enamored of gold trim and the Titan's sneering likeness everywhere, then it's probably quite different from what you recall...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... you have your mother's candor to a fault. You ought have told me everything was fine! The comfort of lies at times is all we have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Daughter. Tell me something of yourself, for I know not a thing. For instance... what is it that you like to do? When you are not... attempting this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What do I like to do? Well... I ensure all of my reagents are in order, so when practicing my craft, what I need is always close at hand. And I like to... ungh, Father, this rings false...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm. Neither the time nor place for trivialities... perhaps, however, at some point... we may discuss such matters more. But there are scores to settle first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Most fear to tread within this chamber, but quite recently the visage of Nyx herself charged in. Saluted in a huff and strode on past! One of Nyx's daughters?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That sounds like Nemesis. She's wanted to get her hands on Chronos practically more than I ever did. Going after the traces of him now, the same as me. We have a thing going.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That almost makes me feel some pity for those traces, then. To have to face Retribution or Vengeance, if not both.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Has Chronos... my father... has he been amenable to your requirements in all of this? No sudden or discernible changes of behavior or demeanor?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's been himself... his newer self, that is. Rather contrite, and I daresay enthusiastic about alleviating the negative effects that still linger here and above.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We ought remain on guard nevertheless. Although, my father never could hide his contempt. If you have rid him of that... then he truly is no longer the Titan I once knew.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How is Grandfather faring in the Isles of the Blessed? I was pleased to learn Lord Uncle Zeus and you were even able to find him an available post there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My father is, surprisingly, quite competent in this newfound regard, at least thus far. The work itself is not too burdensome, although the residents of the Isles of the Blessed can be very demanding.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So Grandfather is looking after the most vaunted of Elysium, making certain that their needs are met, that sort of thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Essentially, that is correct. He is attentive to detail, and not prone to forgetfulness. We shall be monitoring him to ensure he remains that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, herself... even she was powerless against the Titan. Two of Night Incarnate's own sons served in my House along with her... I suppose he has them, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, Hypnos is with us! Fast asleep for all this time, perhaps some sort of... defensive response, for we have found no way to wake him. Brave Schelemeus is with us as well!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos, asleep again? And I've no recollection of that other name... but Time has worn on me. Regardless, if the Witch yet leads the Unseen, the will of Night is not yet lost.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It hurts my heart to see you like this, Father. But, it brings me peace. And strength. Helps that this chamber isn't crawling with Satyrs...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh they have sense enough to keep their distance from me... or perhaps Cerberus. Well if seeing me in this pitiable state should spur you to your fullest wrath, then so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's not quite it; I don't feel loathing or hatred. Elsewhere in Tartarus, perhaps, but not here. Not anymore. I just hope my presence doesn't cause you further pain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No. The only pain... is that your mother and I, your brother, this dog, that we were not... we could not be there to see you grow. Each time I see you now... it fills an absence that was there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother was so proud when you were born. We... did not always think that we would have another child. But we decided to try again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And you did the most important thing parents can do. You protected me. As for my mother, I know her beautiful smile. The way she's looking at me in the painting that I have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The painting, it survived?! The Witch, she must have taken it... and such a risk, for such a thing. Know that your mother's radiance... cannot be captured. No matter the artist's skill.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That skull you have in hand, is that... how did you come to possess it? It once had been entrusted to your mother's care... for her protection, and in a way, yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mother's Aspect of the Argent Skull Revaal deemed me a worthy-enough bearer... but it was Headmistress Hecate who first gathered the Nocturnal Arms for my task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Forged by the Fates! Who so like playing us for fools. That skull did not protect the Queen as much as I would have preferred; but perhaps its work merely is not yet complete.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother sends you her warmest regards, and regrets that she was unable to join me here this time. Certain matters at the House require her attention, if not mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, and please give my regards to Mother, in kind. So you get to be here while she takes care of things there... do you draw lots to determine who does what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Drawing lots is how I wound up in this realm, and is not something I have done again. This is simpler, besides: Being here is how I can best ensure your continued success.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Daughter, answer me something. How is it that the Titan's armies are well-organized enough to siege Olympus, when the Titan himself cowers here in Tartarus?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We have another Titan to contend with there: Prometheus is leading the attack. I don't know how exactly he got free, or what he truly wants... other than vengeance on the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Is vengeance on the gods an insufficient desire in itself? I wanted nothing to do with his sentencing... but your Lord Uncle Zeus, he... well, he would always claim that he could fashion more-creative punishments than I...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The darkness stirs in such a way as I've not felt since very long ago. This cannot be the Titan's work. It must be that of the Witch of the Crossroads, if not you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father: In shadow, I serve; to shadow, I return. Thus have I sworn the Oath of the Unseen, who serve this realm, and execute the will of Night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I know less of the Unseen than you suppose. As it should be, lest the Titan could have learned much more. Then, may you serve the will of Night judiciously...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: This chamber... it once held a prisoner of mine. A mortal king who had attempted to cheat Death! I forced the wretch to heave that boulder up that path forever. He forgave me anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forget him, Father. What purpose does it serve to dwell on some dead mortal now? Chronos wants you to wallow in your guilt. Don't.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You misunderstand. The prisoner, he was called Sisyphus the King. His boulder there was like a friend. He... asked his friend to keep me company. Until more company arrived, he said.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well then I'm grateful to that boulder and the king. Chronos must not have accounted for your former prisoners being fond of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh most of them are not. Some chose to join the Titan's legions and remain here in Tartarus. Not anywhere I cast my gaze, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: This chamber... it once held a prisoner of mine. A mortal king who had attempted to cheat Death! I forced the wretch to heave that boulder up that path forever. He forgave me anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forget him, Father. What purpose does it serve to dwell on some dead mortal now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You misunderstand. The prisoner, he was called Sisyphus the King. His boulder there was like a friend. He... asked his friend to keep me company. Until more company arrived, he said.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well then I'm grateful to that boulder and the king. Chronos must not have accounted for your former prisoners being fond of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh most of them are not. Some chose to join the Titan's legions and remain here in Tartarus. Not anywhere I cast my gaze, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: How... how is it you already have my blessing, Daughter? Is that... you have our family's heirloom... the Jeweled Pom. Where did you get it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... I don't remember where I got it, I... haven't I always had it? Father... I... didn't you... I don't understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Most likely the Witch of the Crossroads procured it, but who can say? My father's influence is strong... Time wears on memory, perhaps. But here... I can add to its power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I cannot believe that I am asking but, how fares Olympus in all this? Even trapped within this chamber, it is evident to me that the Titan's legions are at war.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The surface swarms with the dead, and the armies of Chronos are besieging the mountain. Our family is holding them back. I've been trying to break the stalemate in their favor, but all in all, it's not a pretty sight up there right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Not a pretty sight up there... you mean to say you've seen all this firsthand? How?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean our birthright. Would you believe I learned a means by which I could withstand the surface climate for a while? Long enough to make a difference up there... or to try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Impossible... yet seemingly, all these impossibilities continue to occur. So you must vanquish the Titan, as well as come to the rescue of Olympus. How utterly absurd that you are burdened with all that....

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, I'm afraid the matter on Olympus is more dire than we thought... for Chronos reawakened Typhon, who is very, very large... and dead-set on destroying the Palace of Zeus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! Such a predictable and reckless action on the Titan's part. Although your uncle Zeus may not be quite as spry as when he and that monster previously clashed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't seem so surprised. How did you all defeat Typhon before? I've never fought something like him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor should you fight something like him, Daughter! Let Zeus clean up his mess. His thunderbolt proved quite effective in the past... although you'd think that an embodiment of storms and rage would be immune.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not about to abandon our family to face this threat on their own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Then you're in the wrong place. For if you think thwarting the Titan shall stop Typhon in turn, you're wrong. A monster such as that cannot be controlled. Go, see for yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, I'm afraid the matter on Olympus is more dire than we thought... for Chronos reawakened Typhon, who is very, very large... and dead-set on destroying the Palace of Zeus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! Such a predictable and reckless action on the Titan's part. Although your uncle Zeus may not be quite as spry as when he and that monster previously clashed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't seem so surprised. How did you all defeat Typhon before? My methods thus far have not produced long-lasting results.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your methods? Let Zeus clean up his mess. His thunderbolt proved quite effective in the past... although you'd think that an embodiment of storms and rage would be immune.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not about to abandon our family to face this threat on their own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Then you're in the wrong place. For if you think thwarting the Titan shall stop Typhon in turn, you're wrong. A monster such as that cannot be controlled. Go, see for yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What news of Typhon recently, Daughter? Zeus used to boast of how he vanquished that monstrosity alone. Now I imagine more of a concerted effort is required.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, if you bear some grudge with Uncle Zeus, that's only to the benefit of all our enemies. Typhon yet threatens the mountaintop... he can be driven back, but never for long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We buried him under a mountain once; no ordinary show of strength, for other methods failed. Well, I trust my brother has some sort of plan. He never lacked for those.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...When first we had to fend off Typhon long ago, we dared not think the Titan Lord whom we had already disposed of was responsible. Surely not even he in all his arrogance believed that such a monster would bend to his will!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All Chronos probably expected was that the monster would immediately attack Olympus. How could he have awakened such a thing without our family even noticing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: With Nyx herself amongst the Titan Lord's captives, all that transpires deep within the Earth cannot be known by anyone. And now... even though both Typhon and the Titan fell to us before... we have never had to face them both at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I have not seen you in some time... perhaps you've been preoccupied with thwarting Typhon as he makes attempts to break into my brother's nigh-impenetrable mountain palace.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's a rather accurate assessment of my recent outings, yes. Typhon may appear to be invincible, but there's not a creature alive that likes repeatedly being struck in the face.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: An age-old tactic that served well your predecessors in the greatest battles of their youth. I had a feeling that would be the case; in the end, little changes with time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My father... he visited me recently. Demanding that I tell him where to find you. I made clear I did not know. But you are drawing more of his attention.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're saying he tortured you, that blasted wretch. He won't need to find me; I'm about to find him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not misunderstand... we merely spoke. Granted, that is a rarity in itself. He never knew what to do with my brothers or me... never should have had us at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, please don't speak that way. What even happened with you all...? I know the tales of how you vanquished him, but... know so little of how it was for you growing up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...We often fought. He believed we would take after him. None of us did. And when the Fates decided that the time had come for us to rule in his stead... he resisted. Bitterly. But these old memories... I would leave them buried.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please hang on, Father. I have to safeguard the details, but... I'm getting closer to something... a way to stop Chronos. Forever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Forever... even when my brothers, foster-sisters, and I vanquished him... we knew he would eventually be back... a deathless Titan. But we thought... it would be on our terms. We were such fools.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What else could you have done? He left you with no choice. And you lacked the means to destroy him utterly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It was for the best that we lacked such means... we would have used them and continued using them. I merely judge the dead, or used to... I was never fit to judge the living.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: To think that you might stop the Titan forever. In my youth I could not even believe we were related. Yet now I realize... I have grown old, and wretched, and wrathful... like him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Father, it was my wrath that brought me to this point! And how can you possibly compare yourself? Look what the Titan did to you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We did this to each other! Gods and Titans... doomed to live forever, yet learn nothing all the while. Do as you must... but take no vengeance in my name.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You have the Death Defiance. Runs in the family. Your brother Zagreus, he was also rather sturdy for his stature. Hard-headed, as a boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't speak of him as though he's gone! Chronos has him, and Mother, and the rest. But when all this is over... we'll see each other again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: How can you be so certain? To make such guarantees of the unknown! The Titan... my father... he shall not stop. He is the most hard-headed of us all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cease with this foolishness, Daughter. There is no stopping him. He is inevitability itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You risk too much by visiting me here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: My son would never listen to me; I was a fool to think it a distinctive trait.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I've little strength of late, but you shall have it nonetheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for looking after Cerberus, though look after yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You are as willful as your brother, aren't you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Whenever you return... so does a portion of my strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Take no unnecessary risks and go unseen, Daughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You have vanquished the Titan before; you know full well what it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I am of little use like this. You need not stray from your path on my account.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold nothing back against the Titan. That you are family matters not to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Be on your way, Daughter. I require no assistance here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The Witch of the Crossroads swore no harm would come to you; ensure she keeps her word.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: How strange to have a moment's peace with you and Cerberus, in such an age.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We have anticipated your arrival, ever since the dog bounded in here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Go, take your vengeance. Our family, we've more than enough to spare.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He is no mere Titan. He is Time itself; and Time cannot be stopped...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: These shackles sap me of my force of will; but not entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: This is not how I imagined my daughter would spend the evenings of her youth...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! Another tormentor come to break me, or to try. And such a frail one, at that. Has the Titan Lord a lack for decent help?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You... are you... the master of this beast?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: The master of this beast? Ha, haha! Well, if I'm to be remembered, I could think of far worse ways than that. Though, this beast has yet to so much as snarl at you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: <Gasp> Lord Hades, you're my.... Father, I'm... Melinoë. I'm your daughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My daughter. Melinoë? Merciful Fates...! You cannot be here. He shall find you. He'll find you, and...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I'll slay him where he stands. Where is he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He's in my blasted House! Where you were meant to be, and I. Look at me, Daughter. I could not confront him on my own. What do you expect shall happen?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I expect he'll underestimate me. Father, let me free you from these bonds!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No! No, that would bring greater ruin, nothing more. Now, get out of this place. Though let me lend to you what strength I still possess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! Another tormentor come to break me, or to try. And such a frail one, at that. Has the Titan Lord a lack for decent help?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You... are you... the master of this beast?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: The master of this beast? Ha, haha! Well, if I'm to be remembered, I could think of far worse ways than that. Though, this beast has yet to so much as snarl at you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: <Gasp> Lord Hades, you're my.... Father, I'm... Melinoë. I'm your daughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My daughter. Melinoë. Merciful Fates...! You cannot be here. He shall find you. He'll find you, and...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I'll slay him where he stands. He's in the area, is he not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He's in my blasted House! Where you were meant to be, and I. Look at me, Daughter. I could not confront him on my own. What do you expect shall happen?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I expect he'll underestimate me. Father, let me free you from these bonds!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No! No, that would bring greater ruin, nothing more. Now, get out of this place. Though let me lend to you what strength I still possess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! Another tormentor come to break me, or to try. And such a frail one, at that. Has the Titan Lord a lack for decent help?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You... are you... the master of this beast?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: The master of this beast? Ha, haha! Well, if I'm to be remembered, I could think of far worse ways than that. Though, this beast has yet to so much as snarl at you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: <Gasp> Lord Hades, you're my.... Father, I'm... Melinoë. I'm your daughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My daughter. Melinoë. Merciful Fates...! You cannot be here. He shall find you. He'll find you, and...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I'll slay him where he stands. And not for the first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Not for the first, truly? Then you must know the meaninglessness of the act! Look at me, Daughter. I could do nothing against him on my own. Even though I, too, have fought him before. Slain him before!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry, Father. But I shall not be deterred, even by you. Now let me free you from these bonds!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No! No, that would bring greater ruin, nothing more. Now, get out of this place. Though let me lend to you what strength I still possess.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father, while I know this wasn't necessarily permitted, I thought if maybe it would be of any comfort at all, that I might offer you this Nectar?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Exactly like your brother, blast... I fear I'm not in any mood or state for such a thing. Concern yourself not with my comfort, but with the target of your wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well this is a rare treat. The both of you together in this lounge! What brings you here, Grandfather?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: I yet seek the girl's whereabouts, is what! I have turned over this residence in its entirety, and found nary a sign! She is already as elusive as you always were, Hades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: That or your old senses have dulled. She went running over there by the Head Chef, where she yet lingers in silence. Go, before her patience wears thin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm... what Father meant to say was, no, we've no idea where my good son's little sister is! She could be anywhere! Have you tried checking the administrative chamber? Gods, I sound like Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing here again? I told you, you cannot be here!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I told you I'd be back for you. Though my priority is Chronos. You vanquished him before. Can you reveal to me any weaknesses? Anything at all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I vanquished him with the aid of five other gods! What hope have you, alone? He has no weakness, save his arrogance. And it appears you have that weakness, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then it seems we are equally matched. Father, how long have you been this way? Is there aught I can do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You think I've any sense of time in such a place? And I've said already what you can do: Risk not your everything. Go now, and be unseen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I know not how you withstood the Titan, if truly you confronted him. Quickly now, tell me but this: Have you been... are you well?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Am I... well? All my life I've been preparing for this moment, to bring the usurper of the House of Hades to justice. So, I'll have to get back to you, depending on how it goes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm sorry... I'm sorry. Get back to me if you must. I'd grant your vengeance if I could. But this is all that I can do for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: These blessings are mere trifles, but if they have been effective, so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus sends his regards. The Underworld has kept him busy recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What my father did to Tartarus is a disgrace we shall correct in time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: My father may be different now, but if any old trace of him remains, destroy it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us ensure that nothing like what we have withstood shall ever again transpire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall always have wrath to spare for any remnants of my father's former self.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I would bear witness to your victories if I could, though shall be there in spirit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your work has drawn you to the surface more and more, so it is good to see you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus was practically unable to sit still as you approached.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your brother sends you his regards. He must be somewhere near the surface now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus was quite insistent that you would soon arrive, and sure enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Such suffocating darkness here... almost akin to how it used to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: My father's remnant forces all seem very riled up... as long as this is your doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_02
dialogueLines.text: ...When I was having difficulties with my son... sometimes I thought of you, Father. I could not fathom having had more children to contend with all at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well... I was not terribly successful at contending with you lot, now, was I? Particularly Zeus... to think that I shall see him and the others again soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_02
dialogueLines.text: I can make no assurances about how you shall be received. These dreamlike memories we share did not reach Mount Olympus, it seems. But Zeus does have my full report.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Surely a ringing endorsement! But I merely jest. If Time can be so cruel, then it is only fitting for his children to be much the same. Would you not agree, Granddaughter?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We may have more in common than we'd like to admit. But in any event, Lord Uncle Zeus asked you to come alone. Only the Fates now know what lies in store... although there's still no sign of them, even with Nyx's aid?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: None. I cast them into the abyss. Had I known precisely where, they would have known how to get back. Yet if my hypothesis about such possibilities as we experienced is correct... we may find them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We shall. And I look forward to testing this hypothesis. Perhaps we'll all learn something about ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that we could! Gods and Titans may be doomed to live forever... but we need not be bound to our old ways.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. Erm, Father...? Thank you for all your hospitality. It gladdens me to see you doing well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_02
dialogueLines.text: I would not be if not for you, Daughter. I trust that we shall see each other again soon. And perhaps even you, Father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Likewise, Hades. At least this House is mostly to your preference again. Beyond these halls, of course, much more is to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well... Father, I wish you the best with the ongoing Underworld renovations. As for us, our chariot is here.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_02
endLines.text: That it is. I am prepared.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Melinoë, you're back! We thought we might find you here... well, Hades did. I never would have guessed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Mother and I had looked forward to seeing you again ever since you departed. How have you fared? Are matters in the Crossroads well in hand?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Everything is more or less all right, Father. It's good to see you in this chamber in far better spirits than before, though... why return to such a place? I'd have thought you'd rather be just about anywhere else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The House is partially in order but the rest of Tartarus yet bears the mark of my father's extravagant tastes. Whatever harm he caused our family, undoing his decor shall take longer still!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather shall do his part. It also means I get to say hello.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I shall do my small part then, in the retracing of your steps. Provided I may be of service still. Here.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
endLines.text: Indeed. May it be of use.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How have the two of you been doing in all this? It must be quite a stressful time, having to get the House and Underworld back in order.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it is somewhat less stressful than being imprisoned, fearing the worst for my family and dog, and watching the Underworld become unrecognizable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: It's very different in my case. In this life... one moment I was a new mother, and now I'm not! For what it's worth, you were always an adorable baby, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You could always try and have another child, I suppose? We have nowhere near the numbers on Olympus, besides.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh! Let us not tempt the Fates with such talk, wherever they may be. You and your brother are more than sufficient.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How fare the renovations to the House thus far? It was looking rather far along even before I departed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Appearances can be deceiving, as you must already know, Daughter. The initial work is rather quick. Addressing the details takes much, much longer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Especially if all the details must be exactly as before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: And why should we settle for less? Anything that we did not want changed ought to remain as we desired it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like a big undertaking. Can I do something to assist?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Best leave this one to us, Melinoë. We're pleased to see you here! Your father can administer his blessings and take his mind off of everything! Isn't that right, Hades?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: <Grumble> Just make your choice and go, Daughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Wanted to mention, Achilles sends his greetings and well-wishes! Says he fondly recalls the practice bouts you had with Zagreus... erm, might have had. He and the other Houseservants are doing better lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad to hear it, and please send him my greetings and well-wishes in return. Having known him, at least in our other past... I was concerned he'd take what happened very hard. I mean the attack on the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I think he did take it hard, in his way. His responsibility is to protect the House, but Chronos was too much for him... for any of us. Perhaps gods such as we, or heroes such as Achilles, need the world to remind us to have a little humility from time to time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You're of course so much like the daughter I remember from the other past we shared, or dreamed. But the small ways in which you're different from that Melinoë are fascinating. You seem... more mature. In control.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... I suppose I had to grow up faster in the Crossroads than in the House, so perhaps that explains it. I also didn't have quite as much training in that life. But I'm still me, I think!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I always wondered to what extent we are who we are down to the core, or whether it's our surroundings and upbringing that shapes us. The truth may lie somewhere in the middle.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Hades won't speak of it in polite company, but we have quite the administrative backlog to work through. The number of mortals that perished since we lost the House, compounded by the consequences of the war... it's terrible.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All those Shades need to be processed, judged, and placed... and in some cases located. How are you ever going to do all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll get through it in due time. Chip away as Daedalus might with one of his more elaborate works. And for now, there's plenty of room in Asphodel for Shades looking for a place to stay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Daughter... we had... meant to ask you at some point, about your arm, because... well, it is but a curiosity, and one you need not indulge, if the subject is best avoided...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right! I'm so accustomed to it now that I never thought to mention it. I... had a bit of an accident, a long time ago. Turned out that my grasp over life and death was not as strong as I thought. It's been a good reminder ever since.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Yet your connection to the dead we sense is very strong. Well, we each have our scars from lessons learned... most are not as noticeable or intriguing. Your mother and I figured it was more of a personal choice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You have my blessings as well, you know that, don't you, Melinoë? Unfortunately, I'm not supposed to offer you the more-tangible kind while we're cleaning up after Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Surely my own blessings shall suffice? Our daughter has already faced and vanquished this Titan many times... there is no need to alter what has already been working well.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Simply seeing you two is enough to carry me to the end. Though I'm certain Mother's blessings would have made me even stronger. Power over life and death and such...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, when you put it that way... I suppose I take after my own mother more than I sometimes let on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Daughter... your mother brought life to this realm where it never should have been able to take hold, and we are richer for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you remember how you used to play with Cerberus as though he were your trusty steed? At least he's still getting plenty of activity thanks to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I remember, yes! You helped me get on him and not fall down. He was very patient and gentle considering I was quite rough with him. Where I grew up in the Crossroads, I had much smaller beasts to keep me company.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: The more the better, I suppose. You were always our princess growing up, in that other past... perhaps we weren't quite as strict with you as Hecate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I asked your father to prioritize the renovation of this chamber, if this is to be the place where we meet up now and again. And do you know what he said?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Erm, was it along the lines of Yes, my Queen, I ought to honor your request at once?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I merely said we ought to leave this chamber's updating for last... for I would not erase the memory of how it is. Though perhaps it shall remain this way for ages at the current rate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I wish we could spend more time together, you know? But then I recall how it was for me growing up. Just... don't forget we're here if you need anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Of course I won't. How was it living with Grandmother? She seems so intense, and capable... I like her a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad. Your Grandmother Demeter and I, we had our difficulties now and then, but it's been better since I moved down here. My grand conclusion from it all is simply that children need their space... as hard as it is to let them go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We heard that you were on your way, Daughter. So your mother ensured we each would be here in time to greet you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Way to reveal my entire plan, Hades! We just wanted to say hi, Melinoë. Since we can't exactly have you over at the House for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hello to each of you in turn. How has everyone been? Zagreus, and all the other Houseservants?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: They all are back to work; and well we ought, now that we're done exchanging pleasantries. Do rid us of my father's lingering threat, while we rid this place of his horrid styling.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How has your relationship been with the Witch of the Crossroads, Mother? She thinks so highly of you and Father, that much I know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Hecate was the best handmaiden a Queen could ask for, other than her reticence. We knew she plied her trade in shadow, but with us she was nothing if not dependable.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You say she was your handmaiden... that's no longer going to be the case, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I can find other handmaidens. But there is only the one Witch of the Crossroads, and from everything we gather, she needs you as much as you need her. We're counting on her more than ever, you could say.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We've you to thank for the return of Hypnos. He would not cease apologizing for his dereliction of duty. He now is taking stock of the number of days he missed work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We figured that would help to keep him occupied... seeing as we haven't been able to take in as many newcomers as we once did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The court has remained closed! Nevertheless, there are growing numbers of Shades awaiting their turns, and we require Hypnos to keep them orderly and informed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: He's going to have difficulty keeping all of them at bay if there's no telling when court shall reopen, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, we need not bore our daughter with administrative drudgery. The point was merely that this missing son of Nyx has now been located. One fewer matter to resolve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: That little Pom we gave you. We commissioned a very small number using only the finest gemstones, and always meant for you to have one once you were of age...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, surprise! And thank you. Not only is it beautiful, but it makes me feel more connected to the two of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Poms are said to have that effect. One could eat just a handful of their seeds and never want to leave the Underworld again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad that little Pom we gave you is coming in handy. The pomegranate didn't always grow here in the Underworld... back when I arrived, it was rather gloomier down here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? What I've seen of the surface has felt rather gloomier than here. Though I suppose I've only seen a little war-torn bit of it. Do you ever miss being up there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I miss things about it every now and then. But the Underworld has become my home, as it has your father's. And now your brother's and your own... which turns out to be a fine reason to keep looking after it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's really good to see you both here. Do all the Satyrs and such leave you well alone?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not the ones having to violate the principles of reality as we understand it, Daughter. It seems my father must permit his zealous followers to run free awhile longer to prevent any further misunderstandings between us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: But to answer more directly, Melinoë, the Satyrs and such all flee from us on sight. Often with little goatlike screams!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I know that you are not so fortunate. The least your mother and I can do is prepare you for the final confrontation that awaits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx doesn't always show it, but she's very grateful for everything you've done, you know. You're even using her own Aspect of Xinth... it's a good thing she isn't too prideful!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I often have to remind myself that so much of the world as I know it exists thanks to her... not to mention the Unseen. She's amazing... though must be very busy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, the original reason your father came here at all was to alleviate some of that burden. And to think... presiding over the entire Underworld was only part of her responsibilities. Thank you for helping her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mother, aren't you expected on Olympus sometime soon? I've been traveling there recently... though, you must have a more efficient means than mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: My means was with the aid of Hermes, during times when all the surface wasn't still beset with dangerous remnants of a recent war. And, no... for now, my travels are postponed. We all agreed to wait until things finally settle down.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But that might be such a long way's off... there are times this renovation effort seems like it may take forever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We're goddesses and gods; we're patient, or at least, we're supposed to be. Your grandmother Demeter and I shall find other ways to stay in touch for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: The Skull Revaal... Nyx once offered it to me for my protection, just in case. I never would have thought it would protect me even while not in my care.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you like it back at this point, Mother? Perhaps my time as Revaal's bearer is drawing to a close now that you're back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, whether the Fates already predetermined this or I'm deciding it right now, no thank you. If the Argent Skull has gotten you this far, then you two make a proper team.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You're bearing my Aspect of the Skull Revaal, and making use of our family's Jeweled Pom... I'd hoped that we could aid you, but not expected this to be the way!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father's blessing and the Argent Skull make a potent combination. And I'm reminded of all the times we shared... well, that we could have shared.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Even if that other past is merely what could have been, the memories are real, and I'll cherish them... alongside the memories that we've been making since.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Gold, everywhere, pah! The light refracting from it constantly assaults my eyes. This reconstruction shall be interminable! We're nowhere close to finishing the House itself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: It's all right, Hades. Things were quite recently much worse, and we've weathered worse storms. Why all the urgency?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: If not for my sake, then for yours! These grounds ought to be as you always liked. But we shall tolerate my father's taste a while longer, if we must.... Apologies, Melinoë! Erm, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, we've been so busy, lately, Daughter, that we've hardly had much time to see you here...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's all right, Mother, no need for the both of you to go out of your way. It can be just a quick hello from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: That may be a while as we get our business sorted out. But let's please stay in touch as time permits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: At last you return, Daughter. I was once accustomed to your absence, but more recently less so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: What your father means is, now that we see you more often, it's never enough. Though don't come visiting on our account! We know you're being pulled in a lot of different directions lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well it's really only the two, but yes. These past nights I've been traveling toward Olympus more. Have you been able to stay in touch with the others throughout all this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We are in contact with Olympus, yes. They have their own matters to resolve as we have ours, but... we have agreed to redouble our efforts to maintain clearer communication channels from now on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You've kept so busy, and even still things are bound to be a mess like this for I don't know how long. More than a mortal lifetime, I should think... several, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Restoring the entire Underworld to working order and undoing all the damage Chronos caused doesn't seem like a quick process. But I like easing it along.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: And have we mentioned we appreciate the help? Go on, Hades, give her your best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a strange route you're taking through the Underworld. By now you've likely spent more time in Oceanus or the Mourning Fields than I have...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The direct path down to Tartarus was unavailable so now we're sticking to the one that works. I've gotten rather used to it, and the various unpleasantness along the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll have to keep an eye on some of those places, won't we, Hades? Never a dull moment around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Our daughter, part of the Unseen... officially, Hades and I aren't even supposed to know of you... where you reside... nothing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The protection of the Underworld is a responsibility we all share... but in retrospect, Nyx, in her wisdom, took certain additional precautions, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know how well-kept of a secret the Unseen are at this point. We had to emerge from the shadows once or twice in all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx herself would be the first to say it's useful for the Underworld to instill in others a certain kind of fear. But I feel so fortunate we know you not just from whispered tales of vengeance, but for who you are, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Did your mother and I hear correctly that you and your brother clashed? In the midst of Elysium for all to see...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, Father, it's not what you think! It was what you could call an exhibition match; a friendly challenge, nothing more. Though Zagreus did not go easy on me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: A shrewd decision on the boy's part, then, or more likely it was yours. For all the troubles in the Underworld, this type of exhibition match, as you refer to it, may be exactly what the Shades require.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: A morale boost! I suppose they do enjoy this type of violent entertainment over there. Well, as long as Melinoë and Zagreus are open to it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You trained with the Witch of the Crossroads, he trained with the greatest warrior that ever fought in mortal wars. Who is superior shall perhaps be subject to debate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You fought with your brother again? In another life, I was surprised how well the two of you got on when you were growing up. Perhaps since he was older...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We did get into quite a bit of trouble, as I recall. But I couldn't have asked for a better big brother. He looked out for me, played with me... and now I get to beat up on him!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, no need to go easy on him. Just as long as it's only for show! I'd say we've had our fill of real in-fighting in our family for the foreseeable future.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mother, Father, I'm so grateful to have you back in my life. We've so much to catch up on, but all the time in the world. For now, this is just a small House-warming gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh thank you, this is perfect! Our supply back at the House has disappeared... perhaps Chronos had a taste for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Nectar from the highest peaks... to think we once forbade this substance here, though that was well before your time. Thank you, Daughter. And we have something here for you, in turn. A small token, but a symbol of our family as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Daughter. We could certainly make use of this. Hades had a particular idea for it, in fact.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: If you would permit it, Daughter... I would offer up such Nectar to the Shades assisting in the Underworld renovations underway. As an added token of appreciation for their toil. Is that acceptable to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Of course, Father, it's yours to do with as you please. And I can think of no finer purpose for it than to reward those Shades who have remained loyal to us through all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a fine bottle. We graciously accept! You know, you remind me of your brother sometimes...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: These customs that we share from those above... I ask that you not follow our traditions blindly. Such traditions may be partially responsible for the many mistakes that led us to this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I share your cautious feeling about this, Father, but... of the many traditions of the gods, the act of giving Nectar to those we care for seems one of the better ones, in my estimation.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You ought have many others who would welcome such a gift. To have been raised in the seclusion of those Crossroads... I fear it must have been a very solitary life. More so than one would like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Solitude is not always such a bad thing, but... certainly it couldn't have been easy on you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You need have no such fear. Growing up in the Crossroads was never easy, but... I never felt alone. I always had somebody or something to confide in... I would speak sometimes even to a fountain that we have in your honor.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your words are not as persuasive to me as either one of us would like. If you had grown up in my House, you would have been showered with attention, not forced to harden your hands with toil... especially for one so young.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have never regretted what I had to do, Father. And what friendships I have, I cherish. Perhaps I could have grown up with more, but I had everything I needed. Besides my parents. And brother and dog.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
endLines.text: And now you have us, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know some of the Nectar's going to our loyal Shades, but I would like for you and Mother to enjoy this one yourselves. Whenever the occasion fits, just... sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The occasion for such pleasantries... perhaps after our Underworld renovations are complete, and we have taken proper stock of our resources and the Shades, then finally we may accept a moment of reprieve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, stop it, Hades. Surely we can make good on Melinoë's request sooner than that. You know better than anyone that the toil never ends, so the moments of reprieve have to be seized when they present themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Thus speaks the Queen. Thank you for this, Daughter. But you truly need not spare such thoughts on us. We have each other once again. I myself have want for nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Now that you've had a Nectar for a small occasion of some sort, I did also want to give you this. For a more-special time, perhaps long from now, after we've practically forgotten how it was living apart, and the circumstances call for merriment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: This is... Ambrosia, and with such a hue as could have only come from the source... Daughter, thank you...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
