dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia from the heavens themselves... I scarce remember its savor. But I never shall forget what it was like living apart. Nor should any of us long for Time to take the pain of it away. Let us remember always how we reached this point together.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When I was little, Lady Hecate said something similar... that the pain of the past shall always linger, though assert itself less frequently with age. Well then, perhaps we ought to celebrate the memory itself. An aeon from now, for all I care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: A celebration an aeon from now would leave us with plenty of time for preparation, so... why not, Hades? Ambrosia such as this ought to age nicely till then.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please let me know if I can help with any of the planning when the time comes. Or if there's anything else I can do while you're getting the House back in order.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: It's we who should be asking this of you. When you were born, Hades and I thanked the Fates that we were blessed with such a child. But we had positively no idea...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you're hurt... Melinoë, whoever did this to you, once Tartarus is fully restored, we may have to reserve a special spot for them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's all right, Mother... all part of the process recently. These superficial injuries will all be gone by tomorrow night. Perhaps sooner if I'm fortunate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: All part of the process, then... retracing your steps just like you did to liberate us in the first place. It's all so strange...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We heard of the return of the Three Fates. How much you had to do with it shall be for you to know, but tell us... were they in good spirits, at the least?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They seemed to be. Clotho, who spoke the most by far, she seemed in quite a cheery mood, now that you mention it! So much so that I have trouble imagining her otherwise...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Then we are fortunate, perhaps... for they can be a temperamental bunch. Their moods may not solely dictate the nature of their weavings, but certainly must correlate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We were told that they would cease their weavings for a while. You don't believe it, do you, though, Hades...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No, absolutely not. Although I welcome each of you to draw whichever conclusions you see fit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë, what do you make of this new age the Fates foretold? You know you have some mortal blood in you... perhaps it's for the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We did all right with the Fates seemingly leaving everything to us... so if that's the way of things moving forward, I'm open to it. Though, what happens to the Underworld, if the gods are to hold less sway?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We shall adapt, as we have always done. But unless this new age causes mortals to be relieved of their defining trait — that they all perish, soon enough — then I should think what we do here shall always serve a vital purpose.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you two ever... do you have opportunity to speak with the Witch of the Crossroads... with Hecate, now and again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Given her charge, we're not really supposed to be seen sipping Ambrosia together at this point, though the same is true of you. We do hear about her but respect her privacy.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I see, well... anyhow, I think... that it would mean a lot to her to hear from you every so often, if you don't mind? She fought so hard for your return... harder than anyone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahah, well. I think we can find a way to get the occasional message through the veil, right, Hades? Hecate shall always have our gratitude. She saved our little daughter, after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps we're growing strong as a family through all this. Wouldn't that be nice?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: One of these days or nights we'll have to tidy up this particular chamber, Hades...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Go show that possibility of Chronos whose side of the family is really in charge here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: If there is something more that we can do to facilitate your efforts, merely ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We trust that the Witch of the Crossroads and the rest are lately doing well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Matters in the House are well in hand. We trust that all is likewise well with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We shall always be grateful for what you've done, Daughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother and I expected your arrival, and wish to give you our regards.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Much is to be done throughout the House, but we occasionally need to step away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It is a welcome break from our conventional routine to visit with you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We commend not merely your efforts, Daughter, but the consistency of your results.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Your brother wanted me to say hey there for him when next I saw you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: The House is coming along well enough, though I am glad to get away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you're all grown up already but you'll always be my baby girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Hades, I don't know if these are blessings so much as curses against your father...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: My beautiful daughter... we were just talking about you! How you were as a child, both in this life and the other. Growing up is so difficult... and you had to do it twice.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Which makes me beyond fortunate. Hecate was the best caretaker I can imagine. And I'm so grateful to have experienced a life with you, Mother... where there was so much warmth. And now you've met this other side of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: And I shall eagerly await getting to know this other side of you even better. And, Hecate...? You're certain it's no burden to you... to continue looking after her, just in case?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I swore to you that no harm shall come to your daughter, O Queen, and that vow remains. And I shall always care for her, whether she resides with me or not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Then it's settled...! And, Melinoë... for what it's worth... I likewise opted not to live at home when I was in my youth!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Please reconsider this, Melinoë... you cannot be serious about returning to that dusty glade. Your rightful home is here, and you may be unable to return while you are with us due to laws much older than ours. To serve the Unseen...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...is to serve in shadow. I know full well, Headmistress, and do not make this decision lightly, but... I can't leave the Crossroads. Especially when we've so much left to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Working with Chronos on this new venture of his... you've been instrumental in altering Time as we know it, so you're certainly the best fit to keep things that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ensuring Time keeps working in our favor seems like a good way to spend my own time! It should be interesting, and the matter is close to my heart. Perhaps I was born for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You were born for much more. But we'll do our share. Cerberus has already taken to prancing through the Fields of his own accord, and your father and I shall not be far. As for your brother, well... he always turns up!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All this is going to be an adjustment, though, isn't it? Even if I'll see you all again soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, hahaha! An adjustment is putting it mildly. Now go make your rounds, and I'll see you off before you depart. I love you, baby girl.
